messages.content: I'm about to lose it with Ramirez. Just got off the phone and apparently our countertops are backordered for another 3 weeks. THREE WEEKS. On top of everything else that's gone wrong.
That's incredibly frustrating, especially when you're already dealing with renovation delays. What does this mean for your overall timeline?

It means we're looking at another month without a kitchen. Another MONTH. We've been living like this since December and I'm so tired of takeout and washing dishes in the bathroom sink. The kids are asking when they can have cereal again and I don't even know what to tell them.
That sounds exhausting for the whole family. How has the contractor been handling communication about these delays?

That's the worst part - Ramirez barely communicates unless I call him directly. I found out about the plumbing issue behind the old cabinets from one of his workers, not from him. Apparently they discovered it last week but nobody thought to mention it to the person paying for this nightmare.
A plumbing issue sounds like it could affect both timeline and budget. Have you gotten details about what needs to be fixed?

Not really, just that there's 'some pipe work that needs attention.' Super helpful, right? I'm dreading what this is going to cost on top of the 38K we already committed to. Our food budget is already up 40% from eating out constantly, and with my bonus finally coming this month I was hoping we'd be in better shape financially.
It sounds like you need clearer communication from the contractor about both the plumbing costs and realistic timelines. Have you considered setting up regular check-ins or requiring written updates?

You know what, you're right. I've been too passive about this because I didn't want to be 'that client' but clearly being polite isn't working. I'm calling him tomorrow morning and demanding a full breakdown of what's left, including this mystery plumbing work and actual dates, not just 'soon' and 'we'll see.'
That sounds like a solid plan. Getting everything documented will help you manage both the timeline and budget going forward. You deserve clear communication on a project this size.

summary: Diane vents frustration about kitchen renovation delays, including 3-week backordered countertops and poor contractor communication. She discovers an undisclosed plumbing issue and decides to demand better updates from Ramirez Bros.
